mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving near-term equity outperformance as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with increasing housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue growth expectations and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 60 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth alongside expanding retail sales demonstrates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings catch up to macro momentum.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5 percent annually, indicating that overheating demand triggers inflation fears that outweigh earnings benefits.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percent per annum signals aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite consecutive rate hikes exceeding 0.25 percent, or the negative forward returns are not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-stat > -1.65).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which fuels wage-driven consumption and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of rolling 3-year windows, or the interaction effect reverses sign when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between accelerating real GDP and stagnant industrial production indicates a shift from goods to services demand, which compresses input costs for manufacturing-heavy S&P 500 constituents and generates positive weekly returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually.
